con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
conn: noun. The duty of directing a ship, usually used with the verb to have or to take and accompanied by the article "the."
coo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Comox.
cum: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Cumeral.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
mum: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maiwala.
mun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Munda languages.
muon: noun. An unstable elementary particle in the lepton family, having similar properties to the electron but with a mass 207 times greater.
noncom: noun. A non-commissioned officer, such as a sergeant (army) or petty officer (navy).
noon: noun. The time of day when the Sun seems to reach its highest point in the sky; solar noon.
noun: noun. A word that functions as the name of a specific object or set of objects, such as person, animal, place, word, thing, phenomenon,
nun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anong.
umm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umon.
uncommon: adjective. Rare; not readily found; unusual. (clarification of this definition is needed (uncommon > rare > very uncommon))
